headline: Inference efficiency techniques broke out as NVIDIA cut video diffusion from 50 steps toward one.
body: Ziv Ilan's NVIDIA team reached near real-time video diffusion on Blackwell B200 by combining dynamic quantization, latent chunk caching, and step distillation, pushing the typical 50-step denoising process to as few as one step. That work anchors a cluster of breakout techniques this window: quantization, prompt caching, training-free methods, distillation, and distributed training. Claude continued its enterprise push, with large banks deploying it across their organizations at an Anthropic financial services event and AWS Bedrock enabling Claude Code with prompt caching, content guardrails, and data residency compliance. Robotics surfaced as a counterpoint, with Catie Cuan arguing the coming inflection point should prioritize human interaction over the narrow humanoid focus of 1X and Unitree.

cited_items.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
"ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
